skill.name: ehr-semantic-compressor
skill.description: AI-powered EHR summarization that compresses lengthy Electronic Health Record documents into structured, clinically accurate summaries with extracted sections for allergies, medications, diagnoses, and more.
skill.summary: Compress lengthy EHR documents into structured summaries with automatic extraction of allergies, medications, diagnoses, family history, procedures, and vitals.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 74 patterns across 8 files with a computed risk score of 100/100, but independent AI evaluation determined nearly all findings are false positives triggered by Markdown formatting (38 backtick findings), Python dict.keys() calls (certificate/key false matches), documentation content (path traversal in security checklist, system reconnaissance in error messages), and benign patterns (weak cryptographic algorithm matches on non-crypto text). The obfuscation heuristics flagged legitimate sample data and standard tool operation patterns. Two real low-severity concerns exist: file I/O operations in scripts/main.py lack path sanitization, and a hardcoded GitHub URL appears in documentation. No network access in code, no credential handling, no data exfiltration, and no malicious intent detected. The skill is a legitimate EHR summarization tool suitable for publication.

low_findings.title: File write without path sanitization
low_findings.description: The output file path from the --output argument is used directly with no path traversal validation. The script creates parent directories and writes output to the user-specified location without restricting writes to a sandboxed directory.

low_findings.title: File read without path validation
low_findings.description: The input file path from the --input argument is opened and read without restricting reads to a specific directory. The script checks file existence but does not validate that the path is within an allowed workspace.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded GitHub URL for source attribution. This is benign documentation content and not executable code, but it is a network indicator in the codebase.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated filesystem operations on user-supplied paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The script accepts user-provided file paths for both input reading and output writing without path sanitization or sandboxing. Input files are read via open() after a simple existence check, and output files are written with automatic parent directory creation via mkdir(parents=True). This is inherent to the tool's design as a file processor but represents a filesystem access risk.

content.user_title: Compress Electronic Health Records into Structured Summaries
content.value_statement: Lengthy Electronic Health Record documents are time-consuming to review in clinical and research settings. This skill uses AI-powered extractive summarization to compress 1600+ word EHR documents into 200-300 word structured summaries, automatically extracting critical sections like allergies, medications, and diagnoses in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Compress EHR documents of 1600+ words into 200-300 word structured bullet-point summaries
Extract specific clinical sections including allergies, medications, diagnoses, family history, procedures, and vitals
Process input in plain text or JSON format and output structured JSON with compression ratio metrics
Score sentences by clinical relevance using keyword-based extraction and numerical lab data detection
Maintain original sentence ordering within summaries for coherent, readable output
Run entirely locally with no external API calls or patient data transmission outside the system
limitations: Uses extractive summarization only, selecting existing sentences rather than generating new abstractive summaries
Section extraction depends on keyword matching and may miss information expressed with non-standard terminology
Requires at least 100 words of input text to produce meaningful results
Does not perform medical interpretation, diagnosis, or clinical decision support

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Data Preparation
use_cases.description: Researchers processing large volumes of EHR data can quickly extract structured summaries and specific clinical sections for cohort analysis, literature reviews, or dataset preparation.

use_cases.title: Medical Documentation Review
use_cases.description: Healthcare professionals reviewing patient records before consultations can get a concise bullet-point summary of key clinical information without reading through lengthy documents.

use_cases.title: Medical Education and Training
use_cases.description: Medical students learning to identify critical clinical information in EHR documents can use structured extraction to compare their own assessments against automated outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic EHR Summarization
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this Electronic Health Record document and extract the key clinical information. Focus on allergies, current medications, diagnoses, and family history.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a standard EHR document

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Section Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract only the medications and allergies sections from this patient record. List each medication with its dosage and frequency. List allergies with the type of reaction.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Process these five EHR documents from a clinical trial cohort. For each document, extract diagnoses, procedures, and vitals. Output as separate JSON files with the patient ID as the filename.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Section Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this EHR text focusing on cardiovascular-related findings. Extract sections for medications, diagnoses, and vitals. Use max_length of 400 words for a more detailed summary. Flag any abnormal lab values.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cardiologist reviewing a complex patient history

output_examples.input: A 1600-word EHR document containing patient history, medications, allergies, lab results, and clinical notes.
output_examples.output: A bullet-point summary with approximately 20 clinically-relevant sentences ordered by original document position. Extracted sections include medications with drug names and dosages, allergies with reaction types, and diagnoses with dates where available. Metadata shows original word count, summary length, and compression ratio.

output_examples.input: A JSON object with ehr_text, max_length set to 200, and extract_sections limited to medications and allergies only.
output_examples.output: A focused summary of approximately 200 words covering only medication-related and allergy-related sentences. The medications array lists each drug entry separately, and the allergies array documents each reaction with its description.

output_examples.input: A short EHR text of 80 words that does not meet the minimum input length requirement.
output_examples.output: An error response indicating the input is too short, specifying the current word count and the 100-word minimum requirement, with a suggestion to provide more complete documentation.

best_practices: Provide at least 1600 words of EHR text for optimal compression ratio and clinically meaningful summaries.
Specify only the sections you need in extract_sections to reduce processing time and keep output focused.
Review extracted sections for completeness since keyword matching may miss information using non-standard terminology.
anti_patterns: Using the skill output as a replacement for full clinical record review or medical decision-making.
Submitting heavily de-identified or incomplete records and expecting comprehensive extraction results.
Relying on the summarization for diagnoses or treatment recommendations without independent clinician verification.

faq.question: Does this skill send patient data to external servers?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally on your machine. No patient data is transmitted to any external service or API.

faq.question: What length of EHR document works best?
faq.answer: The skill works best with documents of 1600 words or more. The minimum required input is 100 words.

faq.question: What clinical sections can be extracted?
faq.answer: The skill can extract allergies, medications, diagnoses, family history, procedures, and vitals. You can select specific sections or extract all of them.

faq.question: How accurate is the summarization?
faq.answer: The extractive summarization preserves original text and is highly accurate for what it extracts. However, it may miss information that does not match the keyword patterns used for scoring.

faq.question: Can this skill be used with protected health information?
faq.answer: Yes, since all processing is local and no data leaves your system, the skill is suitable for PHI environments. Follow your organization's data handling policies.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs structured JSON by default, with support for markdown and plain text formats via the --format parameter.
